backend/ssot/lifestyle_registry.yaml — read in full. Canonical engine inputs: height_cm, weight_kg, waist_circumference_cm, systolic_bp, diastolic_bp, resting_heart_rate, smoking_status, alcohol_units_per_week, sleep_hours, sit_stand_value. Alcohol_units_per_week has metabolic+hepatic modifiers (lines 162-181). fluid_intake and fasting_hours are NOT in SSOT canonical engine inputs.

backend/core/pipeline/questionnaire_mapper.py — read in full. alcohol_units_per_week mapped at lines 297-317 and 520-533. fluid_intake_liters mapped at lines 426-438 into MappedLifestyleFactors.fluid_intake_liters. fasting_hours questionnaire field is NOT mapped by any method in this file. extract_behavioural_lifestyle_inputs() (line 500) emits only: sleep_hours, alcohol_units_per_week, smoking_status.

backend/core/analytics/lifestyle_modifier_engine.py — read (200 lines). apply() output at lines 139-146: produces derived_inputs, validated_inputs, input_errors, invalid_input_plausibility, system_modifiers, adjusted_system_burdens. Engine outputs ONLY numeric burden modifiers — no interpretation context tokens, no lifestyle narrative support produced.

knowledge_bus/root_cause/hypotheses/hcy_hypotheses_v1.yaml — read in full. 4 hypotheses (B12, folate, inflammation, renal clearance). None include alcohol lifestyle context. No lifestyle-to-interpretation bridge currently exists for homocysteine/macrocytosis/one-carbon pathway.

knowledge_bus/root_cause/hypotheses/creatinine_high_hypotheses_v1.yaml — read in full. 2 hypotheses (filtration stress, urea co-rise). Neither includes hydration or physical activity context. No lifestyle bridge for renal interpretation currently exists.

knowledge_bus/root_cause/hypotheses/hba1c_hypotheses_v1.yaml — read in full. 2 hypotheses (glycaemic exposure, glycaemic-lipid coupling). Neither includes fasting pattern or dietary pattern context. No lifestyle bridge for glycaemic interpretation currently exists.
lifestyle_registry.yaml is the sole SSOT for numeric lifestyle burden modifiers. questionnaire.json is the sole SSOT for questionnaire field definitions. No duplicate authority detected.

alcohol_drinks_weekly in questionnaire.json:270 maps to alcohol_units_per_week in questionnaire_mapper.py:297-317. hcy_hypotheses_v1.yaml has no alcohol context rule. Gap is real. alcohol_units_per_week is available as a numeric runtime value. Bridge is buildable.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — gap real, fields partially available; scope narrowing required
changes.evidence: daily_fluid_intake in questionnaire.json:348 maps to MappedLifestyleFactors.fluid_intake_liters at questionnaire_mapper.py:426-438, but fluid_intake is NOT in lifestyle_registry.yaml canonical inputs and is NOT emitted by extract_behavioural_lifestyle_inputs(). vigorous_exercise_days at questionnaire.json:305 is mapped to exercise_minutes_per_week in MappedLifestyleFactors. creatinine_high_hypotheses_v1.yaml has no hydration or physical activity context rule. No 'physical work pattern' or 'occupation type' field exists in questionnaire.json — only structured exercise fields. Sprint must use vigorous_exercise_days + fluid_intake as proxy. Cursor must add fluid_intake and exercise_level to the bridge-asset consumption path.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — gap real; weight_loss field ABSENT, fasting fields available
changes.evidence: fasting_hours in questionnaire.json:254 is NOT mapped in questionnaire_mapper.py. dietary_pattern='Intermittent fasting' at questionnaire.json:302 is scored (+1) in _map_diet_level() but not extractable as a structured context token. hba1c_hypotheses_v1.yaml has no fasting or dietary pattern context. CRITICAL: no 'weight_loss', 'recent_weight_change', or 'intentional_weight_loss' questionnaire field exists. Sprint must scope to fasting/dietary-pattern bridge only — weight loss as a structured input is unavailable.

lifestyle_registry.yaml inputs spec does NOT include fluid_intake. LifestyleModifierEngine.apply() does not accept or process fluid intake. fluid_intake_liters lives only in MappedLifestyleFactors (questionnaire_mapper.py:38). The renal bridge must source fluid context from MappedLifestyleFactors, not from the SSOT engine. This is not a STOP but constrains where the bridge is invoked — it must operate on the mapped questionnaire level, not the registry engine level.

questionnaire_mapper.py extract_behavioural_lifestyle_inputs() (line 500) does NOT extract fasting_hours. The questionnaire.json fasting_hours field (line 254) is captured in the questionnaire but never becomes a runtime lifestyle variable. For the glycaemic bridge, Cursor must either: (a) add fasting_hours extraction to questionnaire_mapper.py, or (b) use dietary_pattern='Intermittent fasting' as the proxy token. Option (b) is lower risk (no mapper change). Option (a) is cleaner but widens the mapper file touch.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — new asset layer is correct home
changes.evidence: N-2 architecture §3.2: governed narrative asset layer is the correct home for lifestyle-to-pathway joins. lifestyle_registry.yaml is inappropriate (burden modifiers only). Hypothesis YAMLs could receive lifestyle_context_rules but that extends a biomarker-centric schema with a lifestyle concept — a hybrid that risks architectural ambiguity. A new dedicated asset file (e.g., knowledge_bus/lifestyle_bridges/lifestyle_interpretation_bridges_v1.yaml or equivalent) is the cleanest clean governed location consistent with §3.2. Cursor must confirm the exact placement.

LifestyleModifierEngine.apply() output (lines 139-146) contains no interpretation context slot. InsightGraphV1 has no lifestyle_context or lifestyle_bridge fields. The bridge outputs need a home in the analysis result. Cursor must decide: add a new InsightGraphV1 field (HIGH risk, contract change), add to a side-channel output dict, or make them available via a new accessor pattern. This design decision must be grounded in the repo before implementation — not left implicit.

alcohol_units_per_week: available in runtime. fluid_intake_liters: available in MappedLifestyleFactors (not SSOT engine). vigorous_exercise_days: available in questionnaire/mapped. fasting_hours: in questionnaire but not extracted to runtime yet. weight_loss: ABSENT — no structured weight-change field exists. Sprint must scope glycaemic bridge to fasting/dietary pattern only.
No structural questionnaire redesign needed. Bridge assets can consume existing mapped lifestyle values. Adding fasting_hours extraction to questionnaire_mapper.py is a bounded addition if chosen.
New knowledge_bus/ bridge asset layer is a clean governed location consistent with N-2 architecture §3.2. No adjudication needed.
All three bridges are framed as contextual modifiers and plausibility supports, not causal declarations. Design rules 1 and 6 in the prompt enforce this boundary explicitly.

Expected touch set: new knowledge_bus bridge asset(s), possible questionnaire_mapper.py addition (fasting_hours extraction), possible InsightGraphV1 field or side-channel output. Potential HIGH risk expansion if InsightGraphV1 is modified. Must remain bounded.
N-2 architecture §3.2 and §4 explicitly required lifestyle-to-pathway joins as a prerequisite for narrative compiler build. All repo evidence confirms this gap is real.
